title: Wright, Springfield and Lundqvist to Race Aston Martin GT3 at Nürburgring 24 Hours in Same Class as Väisänen
meta: Published: 20 April 2026 | By Isabella Rossi, Breaking News & Live Reporter | F1CC News
caption: Lukus Wright shook down the white Red Bull liveried Aston Martin GT3 at COTA ahead of the Nürburgring 24 Hours.
body:
AUSTIN, TEXAS — Lukus Wright, Sarah Springfield, and Linus Lundqvist will race together in the SP9 Pro class at this year's Nürburgring 24 Hours, driving an Aston Martin Vantage GT3. The entry will compete directly against the Porsche 911 GT3 R that Eetu Väisänen, Alex Thompson, and Michiil MaraDöner are already confirmed to share, setting up a direct Formula 1‑vs‑Formula 1 battle in the world's most demanding endurance race.

Wright completed a shakedown of the car at Circuit of the Americas on Monday, the Aston Martin running in a striking white livery with full Red Bull branding — a nod to Wright's F1CC ties with Racing Bulls, the Red Bull sister team. The livery, which mirrors the Red Bull corporate colours rather than the traditional Aston Martin green, has already generated significant attention ahead of the June race.

The driver lineup is a blend of current F1CC talent and international experience. Wright, the 2024 F1CC World Champion, is contesting the full 2026 season with Racing Bulls. Springfield, who drives for Aston Martin in F1CC, brings manufacturer familiarity and a reputation for extracting performance from difficult machinery. Lundqvist, the Swedish driver who won the 2025 Indianapolis 500 and currently competes in IndyCar, adds endurance experience from his time in the IMSA WeatherTech SportsCar Championship.

The SP9 Pro class is the premier GT3 category at the Nürburgring 24 Hours, reserved for professional driver crews. The Aston Martin entry will face the Porsche 911 GT3 R of Väisänen, Thompson, and MaraDöner — a trio that includes the current F1CC championship leader and two of the grid's most promising young talents. The prospect of two F1CC‑connected crews racing head‑to‑head through the Nordschleife has turned the 20–21 June event into one of the most anticipated endurance races of the year.

"The Nordschleife is one of those places every driver wants to experience," Wright said. "To do it with Sarah and Linus in a competitive GT3 car, against Eetu's group, is going to be something special."

The Nürburgring 24 Hours will take place on 20–21 June, with over 100 cars across multiple classes. The SP9 Pro battle between the Porsche and Aston Martin entries is expected to be one of the headline attractions.

What it means: A second F1CC‑connected crew enters the Nürburgring 24 Hours, confirming that the famous endurance race will host an unprecedented gathering of current Formula 1 talent this summer.

Last updated: 15:30 CDT, 20 April 2026

[More to follow]
